items.headline: Global Markets Respond to Easing Geopolitical Tensions; US Futures Steady
items.summary: US stock futures held steady on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, following a strong rebound in the previous session. This rally was largely driven by signals of a potential de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict, though conflicting reports on negotiations kept some investors cautious. Crude oil prices, after a significant dip on Monday, reversed course and saw gains today. All 11 S&P sectors closed higher on Monday, led by consumer discretionary, materials, and technology. Attention now shifts to upcoming US manufacturing data and a slate of earnings reports.

items.statContext: Down 0.52% on March 24, following a 1.15% gain on March 23.

items.headline: HDFC Bank shares jump
items.summary: Shares of HDFC Bank ($HDB) rebounded by nearly 2% in Tuesday's trade, snapping a four-day losing streak.

items.headline: Tilly's leads March gainers
items.summary: Tilly's, Inc. ($TLYS) stands out as the best-performing stock in March 2026, with a gain of 190.58% as of March 23.

items.headline: Crompton recommended for selling
items.summary: Crompton ($CROMPTON) was identified as a top stock to sell on March 24, showing a consolidation range breakdown on the daily chart and forming lower tops and bottoms.

items.headline: Campbell's at 52-week low
items.summary: Campbell's ($CPB) has corrected by 22% in the last month and is among the S&P 500 stocks trading at their 52-week lows as of March 23.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Ease, Influencing Market Rebound
items.summary: Asian benchmarks mostly rebounded on Tuesday, reflecting cautious relief on Wall Street after President Donald Trump's statements about possible talks with Iran to end their conflict. This follows a Monday rally in US markets driven by hopes of de-escalation, although Tehran has denied that negotiations took place.

items.headline: Crude Oil Rebounds After Monday's Plunge
items.summary: After plunging approximately 10% on Monday due to reports of eased US-Iran tensions, crude oil prices reversed course on Tuesday. Benchmark U.S. crude gained $3.16 to $91.29 a barrel, while Brent crude added $2.90 to $102.84 a barrel.

items.headline: S&P Sectors Rally on Monday, Energy Sees YTD Gains
items.summary: On Monday, March 23, all 11 S&P sectors closed higher, with consumer discretionary, materials, and technology stocks leading the gains. The energy sector has shown strong performance year-to-date in March 2026, surging 25% due to capital discipline and rising oil prices amidst geopolitical tensions.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports on the Radar Today
items.summary: Several companies are scheduled to release earnings reports today, March 24, 2026. Major companies expected to report include GameStop ($GME) and KB Home. TELA Bio ($TELA) is also hosting its Q4 2025 earnings call. Other companies with scheduled announcements include McCormick ($MKC), Core & Main ($CNM), Concentrix ($CNXC), and Sanara MedTech ($SMTI).

items.headline: Investors Await US Manufacturing Data
items.summary: Investors are closely watching for upcoming US manufacturing data, which is due to be released today, Tuesday, March 24, 2026. This data will provide further insights into the health of the economy. The US Fed Funds Interest Rate stands at 3.75% as of March 2026, with the unemployment rate at 4.40% in February 2026.

items.headline: Magnificent 7 Underperforms, Equal-Weight S&P 500 Gains Traction
items.summary: The 'Magnificent 7' mega-cap technology stocks, including Microsoft ($MSFT), Amazon ($AMZN), and Nvidia ($NVDA), have seen declines year-to-date, contrasting with an overall market rebound. The Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF ($RSP) has shown slightly positive returns, outperforming the cap-weighted S&P 500, indicating a potential shift away from mega-cap dominance.
